hypothesis: Incorporating three-dimensional conformational information—such as atomic coordinates or distance matrices derived from molecular dynamics simulations—as an additional textual modality (e.g., serialized 3D coordinate strings) into the STR-Bamba unified vocabulary will produce a latent space with measurably superior cross-representation alignment and yield statistically significant improvements over the current seven-modality model on quantum mechanical property prediction tasks (particularly QM9 properties sensitive to 3D geometry such as dipole moment μ and polarizability α), because the existing purely string-based representations lack explicit spatial information that is critical for encoding conformational dependencies, and this gap can be tested by comparing clustering metrics and downstream task performance between the current model and an augmented variant pre-trained with serialized 3D coordinate representations.
